intro: C operates at the bare-metal layer, compiling down to fast, platform-dependent binary distributions that manage system memory directly. PHP is a high-level, dynamically typed scripting engine optimized explicitly for web application generation and server-side HTTP lifecycle automation. Transitioning from C to PHP simplifies server architecture deployment, drastically lowers development cycle times, and abstracts system safety exploits entirely.

diffs.from: Static, rigid type checking bounded by explicit low-level byte and alignment layouts.
diffs.to: Dynamic, highly flexible typing supporting optional runtime strict scalar declarations and dynamic evaluation.

diffs.from: Imperative structural syntax mapping closely to assembly control logic and operations.
diffs.to: Web-centric, object-oriented, mixable syntax leveraging distinct dollar-sigil variable bindings (`$var`).

diffs.from: Complete manual control, leaving the developer solely responsible for memory layout lifecycles.
diffs.to: Automated engine-level tracking utilizing reference counting mechanisms optimized for short stateless request bounds.

diffs.from: Multiplexed explicit low-level system polling loops or platform threading models.
diffs.to: Stateless, single-threaded execution per request model, scaling via external process managers like FPM or Fibers.

diffs.from: Basic operating system abstractions focusing on mathematical models, raw file arrays, and terminal structures.
diffs.to: Extensive, high-level array utilities, database wrappers, native encryption drivers, and deep HTTP protocol integrations.

examples.title: HTTP Processing Loop
examples.description: Transitioning from custom TCP socket parsing to abstract global request parsing.
examples.toCode:
<?php
// PHP handles connection plumbing automatically via web server gateway (SAPI/FPM)
header('Content-Type: text/plain');
echo 'Hello';
?>

examples.title: Associative Array Mappings
examples.description: Replacing custom hashmap algorithms or structural lookups with built-in dynamic dictionaries.

tips.title: Enable Declare Strict Types Everywhere
tips.body: To prevent unexpected runtime conversions inherent to PHP's dynamic core, always include `declare(strict_types=1);` at the entry point of every codebase file.

tips.title: Leverage Comprehensive Superglobals
tips.body: Do not parse raw HTTP payloads. Use built-in PHP context structures like `$_GET`, `$_POST`, and `$_SERVER` to instantly extract incoming transmission attributes safely.

tips.title: Abandon Manual Lifecycle Retention
tips.body: C structures require active resource cleanups. In PHP, understand that everything instantiated within a request execution path is systematically cleaned up at end of execution.

tips.title: Utilize Composer for Package Tracking
tips.body: Instead of modifying static system paths or manual binary linking, deploy Composer to standardize your third-party integrations, autoload configurations, and packages.
